behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Real failures here commonly come from actual git graph/time semantics plus PR-state lookup boundaries. MOCK-HIDE: fixture-only logic can miss failures in how the detector interprets real commit ancestry and branch age from an actual repo. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text contains no AI/LLM decision-making, only SCM/PR state evaluation.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo via git subprocesses, create branch/main divergence with controlled commit dates, simulate or query real PR state provider used by production path, and assert detector marks stale only when all three conditions are true.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module wiring: session bootstrap + stale-state provider + warning renderer. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text; (b) no AI/ML output dependence; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM handoff orchestration. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is about emitting warning from setup logic, not validating real infrastructure behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run session-start initializer in-process with a wired stale-state result for current branch and assert warning emission includes actionable branch context fields and expected formatting token(s).

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: This is a single local function mapping input state to fixed labels; MOCK-MISS indicates no cross-module boundary is required. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration+ because behavior explicitly says 'local status-classifier function' and deterministic mapping.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit test over classifier inputs (including edge combinations) asserting exact output labels and deterministic repeatability.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires 'against live repo and PR state', so REAL-INFRA applies (real git state and real PR integration path). MOCK-HIDE: stubs can pass while real command fails on real repo/PR state composition. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/LLM-dependent correctness is described.
behaviors.test_description: Execute `/kaizen-wip` end-to-end in a real repo context with real (or production-equivalent) PR state source and assert rendered output classifies clean vs stale-no-pr correctly.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Core failure boundary is state handoff between branch-state computation and PR-status interpretation (open/merged), which is cross-module but can be tested in-process. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no explicit requirement for real OS/network/subprocess; (b) no AI/ML dependence; (c) no multi-agentic sequence; (d) no LLM orchestration. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text does not require live PR API, only correct classification by PR status.
behaviors.test_description: Wire branch-state evaluator with PR-status provider doubles for open and merged cases and verify classifier/output pipeline never emits stale-no-pr for those branches.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: This failure mode is rooted in real git history transformations (rebase/squash) and ancestry interpretation, which often require actual repository operations to reproduce accurately. MOCK-HIDE: mocked commit graphs can miss edge behavior from real git rewrite semantics. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/LLM decision quality is involved.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, perform rebase and squash-merge scenarios that rewrite commit hashes, then run staleness detection and assert branches are not marked unPRed-stale when merge-equivalent work is already integrated.
